control_arm.value: placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel
control_arm.evidence: METHODS Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.

Total Participants - N.evidence: As previously reported, a total of 1,306 patients were randomly assigned, and 1,305 patients were included in the full analysis set of ARASENS (Fig 1).

Trial.evidence: The trial name "ARASENS" is explicitly stated in the title: "Darolutamide Plus Androgen-Deprivation Therapy and Docetaxel in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer by Disease Volume and Risk Subgroups in the Phase III ARASENS Trial". It is also mentioned in the abstract and the "Trial Design" section.

Treatment Class.evidence: Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.

Number of Arms Included.evidence: The abstract on page 1 states: "Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel."

Quality of Life Scale.value: Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy–Prostate (National Comprehensive Cancer Network) symptom index 17-item questionnaire
Quality of Life Scale.evidence: Secondary efficacy end points included...time to worsening of disease-related physical symptoms as measured by the Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy–Prostate (National Comprehensive Cancer Network) symptom index 17-item questionnaire...

Extracted Value.value: Darolutamide + androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) + docetaxel; Placebo + androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) + docetaxel
Extracted Value.evidence: The two treatment arms are: 1. Darolutamide + androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) + docetaxel 2. Placebo + androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) + docetaxel.

Value.value: Quality of life outcomes were measured and reported
Value.evidence: Study Assessments section on page 3 mentions 'time to worsening of disease-related physical symptoms as measured by the Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate (National Comprehensive Cancer Network) symptom index 17-item questionnaire' as a secondary efficacy endpoint. Additionally, Table 2 on page 8, titled 'Secondary Efficacy End Points for the Subgroups of Patients by Disease Volume and Disease Risk in ARASENS', includes a row for 'Time to worsening disease-related symptoms'.

No. of Deaths - N | Treatment.evidence: The primary end point (OS) was assessed after 533 patients had died (229 in the darolutamide group and 304 in the placebo group).

No. of Deaths - N | Control.evidence: The primary end point (OS) was assessed after 533 patients had died (229 in the darolutamide group and 304 in the placebo group).

Darolutamide.value: Androgen Receptor Pathway Inhibitor (or Androgen Receptor Inhibitor)
Darolutamide.evidence: For patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer, early treatment intensification with a combination of androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) plus either docetaxel or an androgen receptor pathway inhibitor improved survival versus ADT alone in several randomized controlled trials.

ADT.evidence: For patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer, early treatment intensification with a combination of androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) plus either docetaxel or an androgen receptor pathway inhibitor improved survival versus ADT alone in several randomized controlled trials.

Docetaxel.evidence: For patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer, early treatment intensification with a combination of androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) plus either docetaxel or an androgen receptor pathway inhibitor improved survival versus ADT alone in several randomized controlled trials.

Median OS (mo) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: The value is 'NE'... Locate the 'Overall' row and the 'Darolutamide + ADT + Docetaxel' column.

Median OS (mo) | Overall | Control.evidence: The value is 'NE'... Locate the 'Overall' row and the 'Placebo + ADT' column.

Add-on Treatment.evidence: The title of the paper and the abstract state that the experimental arm investigates "Darolutamide" combined with androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) and docetaxel.

Trial Name.evidence: Phase III ARASENS Trial in the title

Full Pub or Abstract.evidence: Presence of detailed sections and "original reports" label

Phase.evidence: Phase III ARASENS Trial in the title

Original/Follow Up.evidence: We examined efficacy and safety from the ARASENS trial for subgroups by disease volume and risk.

Treatment Arm(s).value: Darolutamide plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel
Treatment Arm(s).evidence: METHODS Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.

Metastases - N (%) | Bone | Treatment.evidence: M1b, bone metastases ± LN: Darolutamide (n = 497) High Volume: 386 (77.7%), Darolutamide (n = 154) Low Volume: 131 (85.1%)

Metastases - N (%) | Bone | Control.evidence: M1b, bone metastases ± LN: Placebo (n = 508) High Volume: 390 (76.8%), Placebo (n = 146) Low Volume: 131 (89.7%)

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Trea.evidence: The median treatment duration in ARASENS was 41 months for darolutamide plus ADT and docetaxel versus 34.1 months in the docetaxel subgroup of the abiraterone group in PEACE-1 and not reported for the docetaxel subgroup in ENZAMET.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.evidence: Figure 1 on page 3, under 'Darolutamide + docetaxel + ADT', shows 'Received allocated treatment (n = 651)'.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.evidence: Figure 1 on page 3, under 'Placebo + docetaxel + ADT', shows 'Received allocated treatment (n = 651)'.

Secondary Endpoint(s).value: Time to initiation of opioid use for 7 or more days
Secondary Endpoint(s).evidence: Secondary efficacy end points included time to castration-resistant prostate cancer, time to pain progression on the basis of the Brief Pain Inventory Short-Form questionnaire, symptomatic skeletal event-free survival, time to first symptomatic-skeletal event, time to initiation of subsequent systemic antineoplastic therapy, time to worsening of disease-related physical symptoms as measured by the Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate (National Comprehensive Cancer Network) symptom index 17-item questionnaire, and time to initiation of opioid use for 7 or more days.

Treatment Arm - N.evidence: Figure 1 (page 3) shows 'Analyzed for safety (n = 652)' for the Darolutamide arm. Footnote 'c' in Table 3 clarifies that one patient assigned to the placebo group received darolutamide and was included in the darolutamide safety analysis set, leading to n=652 for safety analyses...

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous.evidence: Metastasis stage at initial diagnosis, No. (%) - De novo mHSPC 432 (86.9) - Darolutamide (n = 497) High Volume... 126 (81.8) - Darolutamide (n = 154) Low Volume
Metastasis stage at initial diagnosis, No. (%) - De novo mHSPC 445 (87.6) - Placebo (n = 508) High Volume... 121 (82.9) - Placebo (n = 146) Low Volume

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Metachronou.evidence: Metastasis stage at initial diagnosis, No. (%) - Recurrent mHSPC 58 (11.7) - Darolutamide (n = 497) High Volume... 28 (18.2) - Darolutamide (n = 154) Low Volume
Metastasis stage at initial diagnosis, No. (%) - Recurrent mHSPC 59 (11.6) - Placebo (n = 508) High Volume... 23 (15.8) - Placebo (n = 146) Low Volume

Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.evidence: N for Grade 3: 248. N for Grade 4: 183. N for Grade 5 (death): 27. Total N = 248 + 183 + 27 = 458. Total patients in Darolutamide arm (for safety analysis) = 652. Percentage = (458 / 652) * 100 = 70.2%.
N for Grade 3: 232. N for Grade 4: 181. N for Grade 5 (death): 26. Total N = 232 + 181 + 26 = 439. Total patients in Placebo arm (for safety analysis) = 650. Percentage = (439 / 650) * 100 = 67.5%.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.evidence: In ARASENS, the treatment effect of darolutamide on OS was favorable in patients with de novo (HR, 0.71; 95% CI, 0.59 to 0.85)...

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.evidence: In ARASENS, the treatment effect of darolutamide on OS was favorable in patients...and recurrent (HR, 0.61; 95% CI, 0.35 to 1.05) metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: High-volume disease was defined as visceral metastases and/or ≥ 4 bone metastases with ≥ 1 beyond the vertebral column/pelvis.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.evidence: and in the smaller low-volume subgroup, the results were also suggestive of survival benefit (HR, 0.68; 95% CI, 0.41 to 1.13).
